steps.title: Understand what weakens 'exclusive to shorebirds'
steps.content: The students claim broken-wing display is “exclusive to shorebirds” (order Charadriiformes). To weaken this claim, the data must show non-shorebirds performing the behavior.

steps.title: Identify the weakening data
steps.content: Choice D identifies two non-shorebirds—the yellow-billed cuckoo (order Cuculiformes) and sandhill crane (order Gruiformes)—that perform broken-wing display, showing the behavior is not exclusive to shorebirds. Choices A, B, and C either describe shorebird variation or non-shorebirds that don't perform it. That matches choice D — D is correct.
